rulings.Case Title: IN RE: RV BRANDAU MARKETING, INC. DBA VISUAL PRESENCE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Amend Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 22, 2013, Line 5, ASSIGNEE HOGE, FENTON, JONES & APPEL, INC.'s Motion To Amend Judgment. The motion is denied without prejudice. Petitioner failed to maintain its evidentiary burden. Petitioner fails to show that Brand Force, Inc. is a mere continuation of respondent or that the two entities merged, or that assets were transferred from the judgment debtor to Brand Force, Inc. without adequate consideration. McClellan v. Northridge Park (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 746. rulings.Case Title: ISOM REESE III VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, AN AGENCY OF THE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing And Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate Form The Suspension Of Petitioner'S California Driver'S License
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 22, 2013, Line 7, PETITIONER ISOM REESE's Petition For Writ Of Mandate from The Suspension Of Petitioner'S California Driver'S License. The petition for writ of mandate is denied. The Court has exercised independent judgment in reviewing the decision below. Upon such review, the Court determines that the administrative record does not support a finding that at the time of arrest the arresting officer had probable cause to believe that the Petitioner was driving his vehicle in violation of vehicle code § 23154. That finding, however, is not relevant to the decision below because there was probable cause to arrest the Petitioner for violation of Vehicle Code § 23152, which justified the other finding made by the hearing officer, which other findings are sufficient to support the administrative decision. rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 22, 2013, Line 1, PETITIONER KPMG LLP'S Notice Of Petition And Unopposed Petittion To Compel Production Of Documents Pursuant To CCP 2029.600 Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, for reasons set forth in moving papers and request is unopposed. Grant Thornton LLP is ordered to produce documents responsive to the subject subpoena and an executed Declaration of Custodian of Records within 10 days of this order. Grant Thornton's production is to be treated as covered by the August 27, 2012 Consent Protective Order entered in the underlying Fulton County, Georgia litigation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARCIA SANCHEZ et al VS. WILLIAM RALPH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 22, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT DIAMOND HEIGHTS VILLAGE ASSOCIATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Diamond Heights Village Association's motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication, is denied. A triable issue of fact remains as to whether Defendant breached the Declaration of Covenants, Conditions and Restrictions by not repairing or maintaining the area in between the floor and ceiling of units 101E and 201E. Defendant has not shifted its burden that a triable issue of fact does not exist as to the source of the water leak and cause of mold in Plaintiffs' unit. Summary adjudication is denied regarding Plaintiffs' third cause of action for negligence. Defendants has not provided the court with applicable authority that a cause of action for negligence should fail at the summary judgment stage as a matter of law because Plaintiff has other causes of action sounding in tort. Summary adjudication is denied regarding Plaintiff's fourth cause of action for IIED. It is not enough for Defendant to show that Plaintiffs' evidence is factually devoid, it must also produce evidence showing plaintiff cannot reasonably obtain evidence to support that claim. Plaintiffs produced evidence that depositions of Defendant board members, Defendant experts, and individual Defendant William Ralph have yet to be taken, which could reasonably obtain evidence to support Plaintiffs' IIED claim. Summary adjudication is denied regarding Plaintiffs' seventh cause of action number for violation of Bus. & Prof. Code § 17200 et seq. A triable issue of material fact remains whether Defendant's decision not repair and maintain the area in question and coordinate the repairs between Plaintiffs and individual Defendant Ralph was unlawful. Summary adjudication is denied regarding Plaintiff's prayer for punitive damages. Plaintiff has produced sufficient evidence to establish a prima facie claim for punitive damages under Cal Civ. Code § 3291(a). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: DIANE CONNELL VS. HAR-BRO OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 22, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT HAR-BRO OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled in its entirety. The complaint pleads fraud sufficiently. Furthermore, while the complaint does not state the terms of the contract verbatim, Plaintiff has pled the contract's legal intendment and effect. A contract should be pleaded either in haec verba or according to its legal intendment and effect. Scolinos v. Kolts (1995), 37 Cal. App. 4th 635, 640. To plead a contract according to its legal effect, Plaintiff should allege the making and then proceed to allege the substance of its relevant terms. See Snyder v. United Properties Co. (1921), 53 Cal.App. 428, 431; Pneucrete Corp. v. United States Fidelity & Guaranty Co. (1935), 7 Cal.App.2d 733, 742. Plantiff has alleged the substance of the relevant terms of the contract. FAC 139. Furthermore, it is generally a question of fact whether a particular third person is an intended beneficiary of a contract. Prouty v. Gores Technology Group (2004), 121 Cal. App. 4th 1225, 1233. Demurrer is not the proper stage to interpret the contract as a whole without evidence of the circumstances and negotiations of the parties in making the contract. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: LAUREE PRIETO VS. B&H EDUCATION INC. DBA MARINELLO SCHOOLS OF BEAUTY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Or Stay Or Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 22, 2013, Line 38, Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Or Stay Or Dismiss. Denied. The service of the subpoena in light of Plaintiff's refusal to provide discovery information regarding URBS was neither a violation of any rule of professional conduct nor an inappropriate ex parte communication. In light of Plaintiff's discovery refusal, Defendant's actions regarding the subpoena were reasonable. Regarding the scope of the documents to be produced, Plaintiff has failed to meet her burden to demonstrate that any of the covered materials should not be produced. Among other reasons, Plaintiff has not complied with CRC 3.1345 or made a comparable showing. Plaintiff's position regarding her motion is not substantially justified. Accordingly, pursuant to CCP § 1987.2(a) Defendant is awarded $5,850 in sanctions against Plaintiff and her counsel, jointly and severally. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA PETERSON VS. G. MAZZERA COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Pltf'S Attendance At Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 22, 2013, Line 5, DEFENDANT G. MAZZERA COMPANY'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Attendance At Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion is granted as follows: The court takes notice of the docket reflecting that plaintiff has filed a motion to continue the July 15, 2013 trial date in this matter, to be heard on May 30, 2013. In the event that the trial date is not continued and remains July 15, 2013, plaintiff is ordered to appear for deposition on a date between May 31, 2013 and June 10, 2013, or by a later date mutually-agreed upon by the parties. The parties are ordered to meet and confer in an effort to find a mutually-agreeable date within that time frame. If the parties are unable to do so, plaintiff shall appear for deposition between May 31 and June 10, on a date and at a time noticed by defendant's counsel. Alternatively, in the event that the July 15, 2013 trial date is continued, plaintiff is ordered to appear for her deposition by June 28, 2013, or by a later date mutually-agreed upon by the parties. The parties are ordered to meet and confer in an effort to find a mutually-agreeable date within that time frame. If the parties are unable to do so, plaintiff shall appear for deposition between May 31, 2013 and June 28, 2013, on a date and at a time noticed by defendant's counsel. Defendant's request for monetary sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TAKESHI YANO VS. TEIKO C. SHIMADA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 22, 2013, Line 31, DEFENDANT TEIKO SHIMADA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled as to causes of action 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, and 8. The Complaint is properly pled as to those causes of actions. Where a contract is ambiguous, "a general demurrer to the complaint admits not only the contents of the instrument but also any pleaded meaning to which the instrument is reasonably susceptible." Aragon Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services. Inc. (1991), 231 Cal. App. 3d 232, 239. Paragraph 4 of the Loan Agreement is reasonably susceptible to the meaning ascribed to it in the complaint. Because the complaint pleads sufficient facts to support a breach of contract, causes of action 1, 2, 4, and 7 are sufficiently pled. Plaintiff has also properly pled breach of fiduciary duty. Furthermore, the complaint clearly and specifically alleges (1) who made the promise, (2) to whom the promise was made, (3) where and when the promise was made, (4) by what means the promise was made, and (5) that the promise was made with no intention of performance. Accordingly, Plaintiff's complaint sufficiently alleged a false promise. C 5, 6, 7, 33, and 34. Accordingly, Plaintiff's complaint sufficiently alleges a false promise, cause of action 3. Because fraud is sufficiently pled, causes of action 5 and 8 are also sufficiently pled. Sustained with leave to amend as to cause of action 6 to allow Plaintiff to plead in good faith facts showing that money is due in light of the clause in the Loan Agreement regarding repayment six months after death of Defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)
